items.title: Credit risk underwriting
items.description: Lenders must ensure features reflect only information available at application time—no post-default collection notes or retroactive bureau updates—or approval models will look accurate in backtests but misprice risk live.

items.title: Clinical outcome prediction
items.description: Hospital readmission and mortality models require features recorded before the prediction horizon; discharge summaries or billing codes created after the event often leak the outcome.

items.title: Demand and revenue forecasting
items.description: Retail and SaaS forecasting pipelines must respect calendar order and use lag features from past periods only; shuffled splits and same-day future sales leak temporal signal.

items.title: Fraud detection at transaction time
items.description: Real-time fraud scores cannot use chargeback labels or investigator notes as inputs; those belong to training labels or delayed evaluation, not live feature stores.
